2026-07-04T10:35:34Z halt-monitor started (pid 2734469, interval 180s, repo /home/jamesstoup/code/james-stoup-agents)
2026-07-04T11:59:37Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-04T11:58:38.984Z [daemon] ✋ daemon-lifecycle-controls halted — worktree kept (step 'acceptance_specs' failed in auto mode (retries exhausted))
2026-07-04T12:02:25Z RESULT: Diagnosis: the writing-system-tests skill committed the acceptance specs (1b1146f) but never executed them, so the RED-evidence gate from PR #181 correctly rejected the step; the ~9-second retries re-confirmed file existence without running anything and the daemon halted. An operator session had already hand-run the specs (9/9 genuinely RED), written the marker, and rekicked — proving no code change was needed. No existing issue covered this skill/marker (#281 is the same pattern but for finish/finish-choice), so I filed one citing #281, #181, and #280.

2026-07-04T22:36:38Z RESULT: This matches exactly: retries within seconds during an active rate-limit episode (`rate-limit-hit` marker written at 22:33:48Z, sandwiched between the retries), burning the retry budget and HALTing instead of pausing/backing off. #270 already describes this exact auto-remediation gap.

HALT operator-park-a-human-placed-halt-must-survive-the -> covered by #270
2026-07-04T23:09:40Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-04T23:06:40.619Z [daemon] ✋ make-daemon-build-push-pr-timing-a-configurable-st halted — worktree kept (step 'architecture_review_as_built' failed in auto mode (retries exhausted))
2026-07-04T23:10:57Z RESULT: Root cause: the `architecture_review_as_built` step's 3 retries fired within ~4 seconds (23:06:34–23:06:38Z), and a `.pipeline/rate-limit-hit` marker (300s) was written at 23:06:39Z — the underlying API call was rate-limited, but the completion-check failure path (`checkStepCompletion` → missing `architecture-review-as-built.md`) doesn't route through the `result.rateLimited` branch in `conductor.ts`, so the retry budget was burned instantly instead of backing off, and the daemon halted needlessly.

This is exactly the gap issue #270 already tracks: "A rate-limited/timeout API call must NOT consume the step retry budget; distinguish 'the API said wait' from 'the step failed'." Filing a new issue would be a near-duplicate.

The build step still hit "retries exhausted" and halted because its binary all-tasks-done check doesn't credit partial completion within the retry budget — the same gap already tracked in #280 ("Daemon halts a build that is making forward progress... halt only when a dispatch makes zero progress"). No new bug — filing another would duplicate #280.

HALT audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre -> covered by #407
2026-07-07T17:30:36Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T17:30:32.846Z [daemon] ✋ fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo halted — worktree kept (step 'build' failed in auto mode (retries exhausted))
2026-07-07T17:36:17Z RESULT: **Diagnosis:** `task-status.json` in the halted worktree has `plan_ref` pointing at the unrelated `2026-03-30-technical-assessment.md` plan (9 pending tasks: "Create specialist agent files...", "/assess SKILL.md", etc.), upserted alongside this feature's own 19 genuinely-completed tasks — triggering "9/9 tasks pending" and exhausting the build retry cap. `summary.json`/`progress.log` confirm the feature itself is 19/19 done and merge-ready; the gate's plan resolution just picked the wrong file.

This is the identical symptom as issue #407 (fixed by PR #410, merged 2026-07-07T16:40:39Z — 38s before this feature's dispatch started). The recurrence traces to daemon self-host engine staleness: the daemon process last restarted at 15:05:49Z (pre-#410) and never restarted again before this feature's `task-status.json` was seeded (~16:48Z), so it was still running the pre-fix plan-resolution logic despite the fix being on `main`. That "an engine fix to the daemon's own gating logic isn't live in time for an adjacent/concurrent dispatch that needs it" gap is already documented (closed only because its own instance recovered on re-kick, not because the code was fixed) in issue #403.

HALT fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo -> covered by #403

2026-07-07T17:54:27Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T17:51:55.129Z [daemon] ✋ audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre halted — worktree kept (auto-parked: no completion evidence after 3 attempts — unpark with `conduct daemon unpark audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre`)
2026-07-07T17:54:49Z RESULT: Confirmed: this halt is the exact scenario already documented in issue #417 (evidence-gate id-grammar drift — bare plan ids `N` vs commit trailer `task-N`), which explicitly names `audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre` as one of the two parked features awaiting that fix. No new issue needed.

HALT audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre -> covered by #417
2026-07-07T23:04:13Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T23:01:17.550Z [daemon] ✋ audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre halted — worktree kept (auto-parked: no completion evidence after 3 attempts — unpark with `conduct daemon unpark audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre`)
2026-07-07T23:07:32Z RESULT: Memory updated to flag that #417/#418 was spec-only and both parked features are actually blocked on the still-open #416 (the `task-N` vs bare-`N` duplicate-row gate bug), so future halts matching this signature should cite #416 rather than file anew.
2026-07-07T23:07:32Z TRIAGE INCOMPLETE (left unseen; will retry next pass): 2026-07-07T23:01:17.550Z [daemon] ✋ audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre halted — worktree kept (auto-parked: no completion evidence after 3 attempts — unpark with `conduct daemon unpark audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre`)
2026-07-07T23:10:32Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T23:01:17.550Z [daemon] ✋ audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre halted — worktree kept (auto-parked: no completion evidence after 3 attempts — unpark with `conduct daemon unpark audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre`)
2026-07-07T23:11:19Z RESULT: Both halts are the same root cause: task-status.json in each worktree contains a duplicate task list — real completed entries under `task-N` ids (with commits) plus a second stale set under bare-numeric ids (`"1"`..`"19"`) all `"pending"`. The build-evidence gate matched against the bare-id set and saw no evidence, triggering the 3-attempt auto-park.

This is exactly issue #417 ("Daemon builds can never satisfy the build evidence gate: agents stamp 'Task: task-N' trailers while plans declare bare ids 'N'"), whose fix landed in PR #418 (merged 2026-07-07T22:59:21Z — just before these halts). Per standing memory, these two are "the parked pair" flagged to unpark only after the #418 merge + runbook — confirming this isn't a new gap, just pending manual unpark.

HALT audit-trail-write-completeness-for-retro-under-fre -> covered by #417
HALT fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo -> covered by #417
2026-07-07T23:14:19Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T23:02:05.101Z [daemon] ✋ fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo halted — worktree kept (auto-parked: no completion evidence after 3 attempts — unpark with `conduct daemon unpark fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo`)
2026-07-07T23:15:26Z RESULT: HALT fix-400-stale-engine-respawn-in-place-stacks-daemo -> covered by #417
2026-07-07T23:24:27Z NEW HALT: 2026-07-07T23:22:16.818Z [daemon] ✋ 2026-07-07-evidence-gate-task-id-grammar halted — worktree kept (step 'build' failed in auto mode (retries exhausted))
2026-07-07T23:27:28Z RESULT: Root cause: build failed the completion check because tasks 3, 5, 6, 12 had no matching `Task:` evidence trailer — not genuine incompleteness, but the exact "trailer coverage gap" class #417 documented (task 3's work landed in a shared commit with task 2; tasks 5/6/12 are test-only/verification tasks with no dedicated commit). #417 explicitly scoped this as requiring operator-verified backfill rather than auto-remediation ("guessed attribution... exactly what the evidence bar exists to prevent"), and this very spec's Task 10 shipped the `evidence-backfill-recovery.md` runbook for it. The operator already ran that runbook (4 local `evidence: Task N satisfied by...` commits at 23:24:58Z) and the daemon auto-resumed/re-kicked the build at 23:25:15Z — the sanctioned recovery path working as designed, not a gap to file.

HALT 2026-07-07-evidence-gate-task-id-grammar -> covered by #417
